name: InputUtil
description: Let a script hear player input. Register once, then receive button presses through OnAction and joystick/WASD movement through OnAxis.

content.text: `InputUtil` is how a script starts **listening to the player** — button taps, key presses, and joystick movement. It has just one job: sign your script up to receive input. Once registered, the input itself arrives through two special callbacks, `OnAction` and `OnAxis`.
Call `AddInputListener(this)` once when your script starts. After this, your script will be notified whenever the player triggers an input.
Step 2 — Receive button presses with OnAction

When the player presses a button or key tied to an **action** (like "jump" or "fire"), your `OnAction` method runs. The important value is `state`: `true` when the button goes down, `false` when it's released.

lines: void OnAction(string panel, string sender, string action, bool state, object data)
Jump();   // runs once, the moment Jump is pressed

rows: Which UI panel the input came from.
The action's name, e.g. `"jump"` — check this to know what happened.
Any extra value attached to the action (often unused).

content.text: Declare `OnAction` with exactly these five parameters and `state` as a `bool`. If you leave `state` out or use the wrong type, your button presses won't register correctly.

Joystick and WASD-style movement arrives through `OnAxis`. The `value` is a `vector3` describing the direction the player is pushing. Read the parts with [VectorUtil](/docs/uniscript/en-us/vectorutil).

lines: void OnAxis(string panel, string sender, string axis, vector3 value)

content.text: For movement, the forward/back amount is in the value's **Y** part and left/right is in the **X** part. Reading the wrong part is a common reason movement "doesn't work" even though the joystick is set up.

Input only reaches your script through **actions** you set up on UI elements (buttons, joysticks). If a key seems to do nothing, make sure its action is wired to an element and your script registered with `AddInputListener`. See [UIUtil](/docs/uniscript/en-us/uiutil) for binding actions to buttons.
Register in `OnStart` rather than `OnInit` — that way the object is fully placed in the scene before it starts receiving input.
